hawkish_phrases: some concern was expressed that the mild scenario might already be outdated
model-based estimates of deflation risks surrounding the baseline scenario in the June 2020 projections had reached their highest levels since the December 2008 projections
some reservations were expressed about the precise timing and proposed scale of the expansion of the PEPP envelope
a number of arguments were made in favour of a smaller expansion of the PEPP
the capital key remained the relevant benchmark for the allocation of purchases across jurisdictions
dovish_phrases: unprecedented contraction of the euro area economy
substantial downward revision of the inflation outlook in the baseline scenario, with headline inflation expected to average 0.3% in 2020, 0.8% in 2021 and 1.3% in 2022
heightened risks of protracted disinflation or even deflation
medium-term outlook for price stability was threatened by the fallout from the coronavirus crisis
further monetary policy action had become necessary to ensure the necessary degree of monetary accommodation
summary: The Governing Council unanimously decided to increase the PEPP envelope by €600 billion to €1,350 billion and extend net purchases to at least end-June 2021, in response to the pandemic-related unprecedented economic contraction and substantial downward revisions to the inflation outlook with heightened deflation risks.
